memberdef.name: MAC_MATCHED_PACKET
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Dest MAC matched with local MAC */

memberdef.name: ARP_REQUEST
memberdef.initializer: 0x0001 /* ARP Request bits in Rx packet */

memberdef.name: ICMP_PACKET_SIZE
memberdef.initializer: 0x62 /* ICMP packet length 98 Bytes * including Src and Dest MAC Address */

memberdef.name: ARP_ZEROS_LEN
memberdef.initializer: 9 /* Length to be filled with zeros */

memberdef.name: ARP_REQ_SRC_IP_LOC
memberdef.initializer: 14 /* Src IP address location of ARP request */

memberdef.name: IP_FRAG_FIELD_LEN
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* Fragment field len in ICMP packet */

memberdef.name: IP_TTL_ICM_LEN
memberdef.initializer: 1 /* Time to live and ICM fields length */

memberdef.name: IP_ADDR_LEN
memberdef.initializer: 2 /* Size of IP address in half-words */

memberdef.name: IP_CSUM_LOC_BACK
memberdef.initializer: 5 /* IP checksum location from end of frame */

memberdef.name: ICMP_REQ_SRC_IP_LOC
memberdef.initializer: 13 /* Src IP address location of ICMP request */

memberdef.name: ICMP_DATA_LOC
memberdef.initializer: 19 /* ICMP data location including identifier number and sequence number */

memberdef.name: ICMP_DATA_CSUM_LOC_BACK
memberdef.initializer: 31 /* Data checksum location from end of frame */

briefdescription.para: The entry point for the EmacLite Ping reply example in polled mode.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the EmacLite device

simplesect.para: XST_FAILURE to indicate failure, otherwise XST_SUCCESS is returned.
This is in a continuous loop generating a specified number of ping replies as defined by MAX_PING_REPLIES.

briefdescription.para: This function processes the received packet and generates the corresponding reply packets.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the instance of the EmacLite.

simplesect.para: This function assumes MAC does not strip padding or CRC.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the checksum and returns a 16 bit result.

parameterdescription.para: is a 16 bit pointer for the data to which checksum is to be calculated.
is the starting location of the data from which the checksum has to be calculated.
is the number of halfwords(16 bits) to which checksum is to be calculated.

simplesect.para: It returns a 16 bit checksum value.
This can also be used for calculating checksum. The ones complement of this return value will give the final checksum.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the Ping reply example in polled mode.

simplesect.para: XST_FAILURE to indicate failure, otherwise XST_SUCCESS is returned.

briefdescription.para: This file contains an EmacLite ping reply example in polled mode.

detaileddescription.para: This example will generate a ping reply when it receives a ping request packet from the external world.
The local IP address is set to 172.16.63.121. User needs to update LocalIpAddr variable with a free IP address based on the network on which this example is to be run.
The local MAC address is set to 0x000A35030201. User can update LocalMacAddr variable with a valid MAC address. The first three bytes contains the manufacture ID. 0x000A35 is XILINX manufacture ID.
This program will respond continuously to a number of ping requests as defined by MAX_PING_REPLIES in this file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ktn 20/08/08 First release 3.00a ktn 10/22/09 Updated example to use the macros that have been changed in the driver to remove _m from the name of the macro. 3.01a ktn 08/06/10 Updated the example to support little endian MicroBlaze. 4.3 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
